title: CircularPatternFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a circular pattern feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: axis
properties.description: Gets and sets the axis of circular pattern. This can be a sketch line, linear edge, construction axis, an edge/sketch curve that defines an axis (circle, etc.) or a face that defines an axis (cylinder, cone, torus, etc.).

properties.name: inputEntities
properties.description: Gets and sets the input entities. The collection can contain faces, features, bodies or occurrences. All of the entities must be of a single type. For example, it can't contain features and occurrences but only features or occurrences.

properties.name: isSymmetric
properties.description: Gets and sets if the angle extent is in one direction or symmetric.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: patternComputeOption
properties.description: Gets and sets the compute option when patterning features. The default value for this is AdjustPatternCompute. This property only applies when patterning features and is ignored in the direct modeling environment.

properties.name: quantity
properties.description: Gets and sets quantity of the elements.

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

properties.name: totalAngle
properties.description: Gets and sets total angle. A negative angle can be used to reverse the direction. An angle of 360 degrees or 2 pi radians will create a full circular pattern.

related_samples.name: CircularPattern Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new circular pattern feature.

axis.title: CircularPatternFeatureInput.axis Property

circularPatternFeatureInput_var.axis = propertyValue

related_samples.name: circularPatternFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the circularPatternFeatures.add method. To use the sample have a design open that contains at least one body. When you run the script, it will prompt you to select a body, which will be patterned around the base Y-axis.

related_samples.name: CircularPattern Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new circular pattern feature.
